title: DNase I Treatment of Purified RNA in Solution for ISOLATE II Biofluids RNA Kit
guidelines:
{"blocks":[{"key":"1hp4p","text":"   Additional reagents required:
   Sodium acetate (3 M, pH 5.2)
 
  Ice-cold 70% ethanol
 
   
 
 
 ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"erhof","text":"Please review the Guidelines under Genomic DNA removal and total RNA purification from all types of lysate for important details.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":35,"length":71}],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"4B6AC12312F3440580EB2A28EC2624EC","url":"https://www.protocols.io/view/genomic-dna-removal-and-total-rna-purification-fro-f48bqzw?guidelines"}}}}
steps: In a micro centrifuge tube, mix together 2.5 μL of the supplied DNaseI Solution, 10 μL of DNase I Reaction Buffer DRB, and up to 87.5 μL of eluted RNA. If using a lower starting volume of RNA solution, bring the volume up to 100 μL using RNase-free water.
Gently swirl tube to mix solution.
Gently spin down (approx. 1s at 1000 x g) to collect solution at bottom of tube.
Incubate at room temperature (18-25°C) for 10 min. 00:10:00
Repurify the RNA with a suitable RNA clean-up procedure, e.g. using ethanol precipitation below.
Add 1/10th volume of sodium acetate (3 M, pH 5.2)
Add between 2.5 and 3 volumes of 96-100% ethanol to one volume of sample. Mix thoroughly.

Precipitate for one hour at -20°C or overnight at -20°C. 01:00:00 Note: Choose longer incubation times if the sample has a low RNA concentration. Shorter incubation times are sufficient for high RNA concentrations.
Centrifuge at maximum speed for 10 min. 00:10:00
Wash the RNA pellet with ice-cold 70% ethanol.
Dry the RNA pellet and resuspend the RNA in RNase-free water.
creator: Steve Hawkins
